metadata.title: Paediatric addendum to CHMP guideline on clinical investigation of medicinal products in the treatment of lipid disorders

rules.rule_text: This addendum must be read in conjunction with the adult Guideline on Clinical Investigation of Medicinal Products in the Treatment of Lipid Disorders (EMA/CPMP/3020/2003).
rules.verbatim_quote: This is an addendum to the Guideline on Clinical Investigation of Medicinal Products in the Treatment of Lipid Disorders (EMA/CPMP/3020/2003). It is not meant as a guidance document on its own but rather highlights differences from adult patients with lipid disorders and points out specific paediatric issues.

rules.rule_text: Efficacy and safety should be established in adults before children are included in trials investigating lipid disorders.
rules.verbatim_quote: In general, efficacy and safety should be established in adults, before children are included in trials investigating lipid disorders.
rules.exceptions: Extrapolation from adults to children is not straightforward due to differences in lipid disorder type, target lipid levels, surrogate endpoints, and specific safety issues.

rules.rule_text: The addendum focuses on hypercholesterolemia in children with primary lipid disorders. This is the primary scope for paediatric clinical investigation.
rules.verbatim_quote: Similar to the Guideline on Clinical Investigation of Medicinal Products in the Treatment of Lipid Disorders (EMA/CPMP/3020/2003), this addendum will focus on hypercholesterolemia, in particular in children with primary lipid disorders.

rules.rule_text: Beneficial effects on cardiovascular outcome must be extrapolated from adult studies if available. Post-marketing observational studies should be part of the follow-up plan when paediatric use is approved on the basis of surrogate endpoints.
rules.verbatim_quote: the beneficial effects on cardiovascular outcome have to be extrapolated from studies in adults, if available. However, observational studies after marketing may provide additional information and should be part of the follow-up plan once paediatric use is approved on the basis of surrogate endpoint indicators for lipid levels as well as for vascular damage.

rules.rule_text: In young children, lowering LDL-cholesterol to <=3.5 mmol/L may be a sufficient target to reverse vascular damage.
rules.verbatim_quote: In young children lowering LDL-cholesterol to <= 3.5 mmol/L might be sufficient to reverse vascular damage.
rules.exceptions: Whether further lowering results in further morbidity and mortality reduction without compromising cholesterol synthesis in growing children is currently unknown.

rules.rule_text: Other lipid parameters, in particular HDL-cholesterol, may be included as primary or secondary endpoints. Age/gender specific reference values should be applied where indicated (6-18 years).
rules.verbatim_quote: Other lipid parameters, in particular HDL-cholesterol, may be included as primary of secondary end points. They may be predictive of vascular changes as well but their correlation with cardiovascular outcome needs further validation. The age/gender specific reference values should be applied where indicated (6-18 years).

rules.rule_text: Atherosclerosis progression can be evaluated in young children by carotid intima-media thickness (cIMT) as a measure of vascular damage.
rules.verbatim_quote: Atherosclerosis progression can be evaluated in young children by carotid intima-media thickness (cIMT).
rules.exceptions: Below age 18, vascular abnormalities may be completely reversible due to unloading of lipid from macrophages in the arterial wall.

rules.rule_text: A full lipoprotein profile should be included in vascular damage evaluation, as the relationship between vascular damage and LDL cholesterol levels may be variable.
rules.verbatim_quote: The relationship between vascular damage and LDL cholesterol levels may be variable to some extent and an inclusion of a full lipoprotein profile may provide further information.

rules.rule_text: Diagnosis and classification of primary lipid disorders in children (HeFH, HoFH, FDB) should be based on LDL-cholesterol levels, family history, and where indicated, genetic analysis.
rules.verbatim_quote: Criteria for diagnosis and classification of primary lipid disorders in children, in particular in homo- and heterozygous familial hypercholesterolaemia (HeFH) and familial defective apolipoprotein B (FDB) in children, should be based on LDL-cholesterol levels, family history, and, if indicated (e.g. homozygous hypercholesterolemia), supported by genetic analysis.

rules.rule_text: In children with genetic hypoalphaproteinemia, diagnosis should be based on HDL-levels.
rules.verbatim_quote: Additionally, in children with genetic hypoalphaproteinemia, diagnosis should be based on HDL-levels.

rules.rule_text: Treatment benefit in genetic, low HDL-cholesterol disorders should be studied first in adults before including children.
rules.verbatim_quote: Treatment benefit in genetic, low HDL-cholesterol disorders should be studied first in adults before including children.

rules.rule_text: When conducting studies during adolescence, age, ethnic background and gender differences must be taken into account. Cholesterol levels are lower during growth spurt.
rules.verbatim_quote: When conducting studies during adolescence, the age, ethnic background and gender differences should be taken into account. Cholesterol levels are lower during growth spurt.

rules.rule_text: Dietary and lifestyle intervention must be initiated prior to a pharmacological intervention study, for both primary and secondary lipid disorders.
rules.verbatim_quote: Dietary and lifestyle intervention should be initiated prior to a pharmacological intervention study.

rules.rule_text: For secondary lipid disorders, diagnostic criteria must also take into account underlying cause, concomitant treatment, ethnic background and gender differences.
rules.verbatim_quote: These criteria should also take into account underlying cause, concomitant treatment, ethnic background and gender differences. Dietary and lifestyle intervention should be initiated prior to a pharmacological intervention study.

rules.rule_text: Development of special paediatric formulations is encouraged. Tablet or capsule size may be important. PK data should be provided for the claimed age group.
rules.verbatim_quote: The development of special paediatric formulations is encouraged, as appropriate. Tablet or capsule size may be important in this regard. Pharmacokinetic data should be provided for the claimed age group.

rules.rule_text: Placebo-controlled studies are not always acceptable or feasible in children (e.g., homozygous hypercholesterolemia). This must be discussed by the sponsor.
rules.verbatim_quote: Placebo-controlled studies as suggested in the adult guideline are not always acceptable or feasible in children, for instance in patients with homozygous hypercholesterolemia. This should be discussed by the sponsor.

rules.rule_text: Confirmatory therapeutic studies should be controlled studies lasting 3 to 6 months, usually followed by an open-label extension for 1 year. The applicant must provide a rationale for sample size calculation.
rules.verbatim_quote: These will mostly be controlled studies lasting three to six months that will usually be followed by an open label extension for 1 year. The applicant should provide a rationale for the sample size calculation.

rules.rule_text: Lipid levels are the primary endpoints in confirmatory studies; vascular damage investigations should be supportive secondary endpoints.
rules.verbatim_quote: Lipid levels are the primary endpoints, whereas the investigations of vascular damage should be supportive secondary endpoints.

rules.rule_text: Below the age of 6 years, patients with HoFH will be studied for PK/PD only (not confirmatory efficacy).
rules.verbatim_quote: Below the age of 6 years, patients with HoFH will be studied for PK/PD only.

rules.rule_text: Children are eligible for investigation of new drug classes as add-on treatment in case of insufficient response to current treatment (in particular statins). When add-on efficacy is demonstrated sufficiently, a withdrawal design or comparative trial with monotherapy may be initiated.
rules.verbatim_quote: Children are eligible for investigation of drugs of a new class of agents as add-on treatment in case of insufficient response to current treatment, in particular statins. When efficacy as add-on therapy has been shown sufficiently, a withdrawal design for statins and/or comparative trial(s) with monotherapy may be initiated.
rules.exceptions: Inclusion in monotherapy trials can also be considered when other therapy has failed or is not tolerated.

rules.rule_text: Safety monitoring must include assessment of growth, cognitive development, sexual maturity, muscular and liver enzyme levels. Acceptable parameters include muscular cramps, auxological parameters, sexual development, biochemical liver toxicity, hormonal status (steroids, FSH, LH, estradiol, testosterone, ACTH, DHEAS, cortisol), and muscle enzymes (creatinine phosphokinase).
rules.verbatim_quote: Long-term issues in relation to growth, cognitive development and sexual maturity are of particular importance, as well as changes in muscular and liver enzyme levels (similar to adults). Acceptable parameters are clinical effects, including muscular cramps, auxological parameters, sexual development, biochemical, liver toxicity (transaminases), hormonal status (steroids, follicle-stimulating hormone, luteinizing hormone, estradiol, testosterone, adrenocorticotropic hormone, dehydroepiandrosterone sulfate, cortisol), muscle enzymes (creatinine phosphokinase).

rules.rule_text: A follow-up of at least two years is recommended for drugs of a new class of agents. Follow-up cohorts after marketing must provide data on cardiovascular outcome. HDL-cholesterol raising drugs must be followed for changes in steroid hormone profiles and their biological actions.
rules.verbatim_quote: A follow-up of at least two years is recommended for drugs of a new class of agents. Follow-up cohorts after marketing will provide additional information, including data on cardiovascular outcome. Treatment with the HDL-cholesterol raising drugs should be followed for changes in steroid hormone profiles and their biological actions.
